intro: Migrating from JavaScript to Kotlin shifts your system from a highly dynamic script environment to a modern, type-safe multiplatform language executing on the JVM or native binaries. This pipeline transition enhances codebase maintainability and eradicates common null-pointer runtime crashes via explicit compilation validation rules. Developers gain deep object safety constraints without sacrificing modern, concise language ergonomics.

diffs.from: Dynamic type models relying on runtime evaluation and loosely structured object layouts.
diffs.to: Static strong typing with explicit nullability tracking baked completely into the compiler constraints.

diffs.from: Flexible script layout allowing unbracketed operations, variable re-declarations, and implicit statements.
diffs.to: Expressive, concise layout requiring explicit scope block validation while removing legacy boilerplate overhead like semicolons.

diffs.from: Engine-driven automatic garbage collection optimized for UI interfaces and browser loops.
diffs.to: Managed JVM runtime garbage execution profiles enhanced with lightweight compilation tracking routines like inline optimizations.

diffs.from: Single-threaded event loop pipeline invoking promises and microtask schedules.
diffs.to: Compiler-managed coroutine architectures executing non-blocking asynchronous routines natively across multi-core paths.

diffs.from: Core library managing runtime variables, string parsing, and web platform primitives.
diffs.to: Rich collection extensions, functional pipeline filters, and cross-platform multi-threaded utility libraries.

examples.title: Null Containment Logic
examples.description: Transforming runtime verification conditions into compile-time null-safe expressions.

examples.title: Collection Transformation
examples.description: Mapping functional array tracking mutations into concise Kotlin collection operations.

tips.title: Map Variables to Val or Var
tips.body: Enforce strict state rules by declaring read-only properties with 'val' and mutable data pipelines with 'var'.

tips.title: Utilize Named Function Parameters
tips.body: Replicate dynamic configurations cleanly by utilizing Kotlin's native named arguments and default parameters.

tips.title: Replace Callbacks with Coroutines
tips.body: Convert nested callback chains or promise chains into sequential, highly readable async blocks using suspending functions.

tips.title: Adopt Smart Casting
tips.body: Stop manual variable casting. Once you verify an object type with Kotlin's 'is' operator, the compiler auto-casts it dynamically.

faqs.question: Can Kotlin run on the frontend like JavaScript?
faqs.answer: Yes. Kotlin Multiplatform can compile directly down to JavaScript backend targets or transpile into native web execution modules.

faqs.question: What is the equivalent of a JavaScript Object literal?
faqs.answer: For simple map needs use mapOf(), or use Kotlin data classes to model structured records safely.

faqs.question: How do I handle global variables safely?
faqs.answer: Avoid open global variables. Define variables within package structures as top-level declarations or encapsulate them within top-level objects.
